entry.title: Orion Belt vs Teleport (and Boundary / bastions)
entry.summary: Short version: Orion Belt is a self-hosted SSH access gateway with PAM workflows. Teleport is a broad zero-trust access platform. If you mainly need mediated SSH, recording, JIT, and MFA — without inbound ports on every host or a large ops surface — Orion Belt is the lighter fit.
entry.content:
Short version: Orion Belt is a self-hosted SSH access gateway with PAM workflows. Teleport is a broad zero-trust access platform. If you mainly need mediated SSH, recording, JIT, and MFA — without inbound ports on every host or a large ops surface — Orion Belt is the lighter fit.

License

Orion Belt is Apache 2.0 + Commons Clause (free to self-host/use internally; not for sale as a product/service). Teleport’s licensing is separate — verify current terms for your use case.

Side-by-side​

DimensionOrion BeltTeleportBoundaryTraditional bastion
Primary jobSSH PAM / bastion gatewayMulti-protocol zero-trustIdentity-aware brokeringNetwork jump point
ProtocolsSSH / SCP (focused)SSH, K8s, DB, web, …Many via workersWhatever you tunnel
Target reachAgents dial outNode agents / tunnelsWorkers / proxiesOften inbound
Session recordingYes (+ live watch)YesYes (with path)DIY
JIT / approvalsBuilt-in (+ ChatOps)Access requestsIdP / workflowsRare
Ops weightSmaller SSH sliceLarge platformMedium–largeSmall, feature-poor
HostingSelf-hostedSelf-hosted or cloudSelf-hosted or HCPSelf-hosted

Architecture contrast​

Orion Belt: Operator → Gateway ← (reverse SSH) ← Agent on target

The target initiates the tunnel. The gateway brokers policy, recording, and live watch.

Traditional bastion: still often means keys, little recording/JIT, and inbound exposure somewhere on the path.

Teleport-shaped platforms: powerful and broad — correspondingly more to deploy and operate.

When to choose Orion Belt​

  • Privileged access is mostly SSH
  • You want no inbound SSH on app hosts
  • You want recording + live watch + JIT without a PAM appliance
  • You prefer self-hosted, Linux-first, inspectable code
  • You’re OK with a narrower scope today (OIDC/HA and more protocols are on the roadmap)

When Teleport (or similar) fits better​

  • You need first-class K8s / DB / Windows / web access in one product now
  • You’re already standardized on that platform org-wide

Security tradeoff (reverse agents)​

Gain: targets need outbound reachability to the gateway, not inbound SSH.
Cost: offline agents aren’t reachable; the gateway is a high-value control plane — harden it.

Try before you debate​

Try Orion Belt in 10 minutes · GitHub · Discord

entry.title: Orion Belt v1.0 is out
entry.summary: Orion Belt v1.0 is a self-hosted SSH access gateway with PAM workflows: reverse agents, session recording, live watch, JIT approvals, MFA/WebAuthn, ReBAC, and optional SSH CA.
entry.content:
Orion Belt v1.0 is a self-hosted SSH access gateway with PAM workflows: reverse agents, session recording, live watch, JIT approvals, MFA/WebAuthn, ReBAC, and optional SSH CA.

Positioning​

Teleport-like SSH access control — without opening inbound ports on every host or adopting a large platform.

Try it​

About ten minutes to a recorded session:

Try Orion Belt in 10 minutes

git clone https://github.com/orion-belt-dev/orion-belt.git
cd orion-belt
./scripts/docker-quickstart.sh

License​

Apache 2.0 + Commons Clause — free to self-host and use internally; you cannot sell Orion Belt as a product or hosted service.

Community​

  • Discord
  • GitHub
  • Looking for early operators — join Discord or open a Discussion
